originalAlarm.description: Branch condition evaluates to a garbage value

sanitizedDescription: Branch condition evaluates to a garbage value
presenceCondition:
(or (and CONFIG_SLOB (not CONFIG_FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER) CONFIG_SLOB)
    (and (not CONFIG_SLOB) (not CONFIG_FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER) (not CONFIG_SLOB)))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_SLOB () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 0f8f8094d28.c L36 (mapped from desugared L71): 'Branch condition evaluates to a garbage value' at `if (!cache)`. PC: !CONFIG_FORCE_MAX_ZONEORDER (SLOB branches cancel out). CONFIG_SLOB=T paths are non-compilable in PRODUCT (kmalloc_caches undeclared). CONFIG_SLOB=F: kmalloc_caches is a globally declared array which is zero-initialized in C (all pointers NULL), so cache = kmalloc_caches[i] = NULL and `if (!cache)` evaluates to true, not garbage. The alarm in the SLOB=T desugared path is a desugaring artifact; in SLOB=F the alarm is a false positive (global zero-initialization not modeled).
